description: CLI interface for Manus API v2

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

options.name: --force
options.help: Clear existing credentials and re-authenticate

login.usage_instructions: Use to set up Manus API credentials. Use --profile/-p to save to a specific profile. Use --force/-F to clear and re-authenticate. Use --credential-type/-c to authenticate only a specific credential type (e.g., 'oauth', 'browser_session').

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Use to clear stored API credentials. Use --profile/-p to clear credentials for a specific profile.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

status.usage_instructions: Use to verify credentials are configured. Use --profile/-p to check a specific profile. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

options.name: --verbose
options.help: Show detailed checks

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name (defaults to all profiles)

test.usage_instructions: Use to verify that stored credentials actually work against the API. Use --verbose/-v for detailed checks. Use --profile/-p to test a specific profile.

list.help: List all profiles and show their auth types and active state.

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter: field:op:value (e.g., active:eq:True)

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to browse all profiles. Use --filter/-f to find specific profiles (e.g., active:eq:True). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

get.usage_instructions: Use to view full details of a profile by NAME. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new profile. NAME is positional and required (e.g., 'staging', 'production'). Creates from .env.example template.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

select.usage_instructions: Use `auth profiles select NAME` to activate NAME within its auth type. Other auth types keep their active profiles unchanged.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.

options.name: --force
options.help: Skip confirmation

delete.usage_instructions: Use to permanently delete a profile. NAME is positional and required. Use --force/-F to skip the confirmation prompt.

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

options.name: --force
options.help: Skip confirmation

remove.usage_instructions: Use to permanently delete a profile. NAME is positional and required. Use --force/-F to skip the confirmation prompt.

profiles.usage_instructions: Profile management -- list, get, create, select, and delete configuration profiles. Profiles allow multiple environments (e.g., staging, production) with separate credentials.

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management -- login, logout, check status, refresh tokens, and test credentials. Supports multiple profiles via --profile/-p. Always check `auth status` before running task operations.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

create.help: Create a new Manus AI task using the v2 API.

arguments.name: prompt
arguments.help: Prompt text for the initial task message

options.name: --prompt-file
options.help: Read prompt text from a file

options.name: --content-part
options.help: Additional message content part as JSON object or array of objects

options.name: --agent-profile
options.help: Manus agent profile (manus-1.6, manus-1.6-lite, manus-1.6-max)

options.name: --project-id
options.help: Associate the task with a project ID

options.name: --interactive
options.help: --no-interactive           Allow the agent to pause and ask follow-up questions

options.name: --wait
options.help: --no-wait                  Wait for a terminal status update

options.name: --timeout
options.help: Max seconds to wait for completion

options.name: --poll
options.help: Seconds between status checks

options.name: --share-visibility
options.help: Task visibility (private, team, public)

options.name: --hide
options.help: Hide the task from the Manus webapp task list

options.name: --locale
options.help: Output locale (for example: en, zh-CN, ja)

options.name: --title
options.help: Custom task title

options.name: --connector
options.help: Connector ID to enable for the task

options.name: --enable-skill
options.help: Skill ID to enable for the task

options.name: --force-skill
options.help: Skill ID the agent must invoke

options.name: --structured-output-schema
options.help: Structured output JSON schema

options.name: --structured-output-schema-file
options.help: Read structured output JSON schema from a file

options.name: --quiet
options.help: Suppress status messages

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

create.usage_instructions: Create a Manus API v2 task. PROMPT is optional when using `--prompt-file` or `--content-part`. Use `--agent-profile/-a` for manus-1.6, manus-1.6-lite, or manus-1.6-max. By default waits for a terminal status update; use `--no-wait` for async creation.

send.help: Send a follow-up message to an existing task.

arguments.name: task_id
arguments.help: Task ID to send the message to

options.name: --prompt-file
options.help: Read prompt text from a file

options.name: --content-part
options.help: Additional message content part as JSON object or array of objects

options.name: --agent-profile
options.help: Optional Manus agent profile override for this turn

options.name: --wait
options.help: --no-wait           Wait for a terminal status update

options.name: --timeout
options.help: Max seconds to wait for completion

options.name: --poll
options.help: Seconds between status checks

options.name: --connector
options.help: Connector ID to enable for this turn

options.name: --enable-skill
options.help: Skill ID to enable for this turn

options.name: --force-skill
options.help: Skill ID the agent must invoke during this turn

options.name: --structured-output-schema
options.help: Structured output JSON schema

options.name: --structured-output-schema-file
options.help: Read structured output JSON schema from a file

options.name: --quiet
options.help: Suppress status messages

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

send.usage_instructions: Send a follow-up message to an existing task through `task.sendMessage`. Use `--agent-profile/-a` only to override the task agent profile for this turn. By default waits for a terminal status update; use `--no-wait` for async sends.

continue.help: Hidden compatibility alias for task send.

options.name: --prompt-file
options.help: Read prompt text from a file

options.name: --content-part
options.help: Additional message content part as JSON object or array of objects

options.name: --agent-profile
options.help: Optional Manus agent profile override

options.name: --wait
options.help: --no-wait           Wait for a terminal status update

options.name: --timeout
options.help: Max seconds to wait for completion

options.name: --poll
options.help: Seconds between status checks

options.name: --connector
options.help: Connector ID to enable for this turn

options.name: --enable-skill
options.help: Skill ID to enable for this turn

options.name: --force-skill
options.help: Skill ID the agent must invoke

options.name: --structured-output-schema
options.help: Structured output JSON schema

options.name: --structured-output-schema-file
options.help: Read structured output JSON schema from a file

options.name: --quiet
options.help: Suppress status messages

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

continue.usage_instructions: Compatibility alias for `manus task send`. Use the same arguments and options, including `--agent-profile/-a` for the Manus agent profile override.

get.help: Get task metadata and current status.

options.name: --table
options.help: Display the task as a formatted table

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

get.usage_instructions: Use to retrieve the current task object from `task.detail` by TASK_ID. This returns task metadata and status, not the full event history. Use `task messages` for conversation output and status events. Use `--table/-t` for formatted output.

wait.help: Wait for a task to stop or pause for confirmation.

options.name: --timeout
options.help: Max seconds to wait

options.name: --poll
options.help: Seconds between status checks

options.name: --verbose-messages
options.help: Include verbose message events while polling

options.name: --quiet
options.help: Suppress status messages

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

wait.usage_instructions: Use to poll `task.listMessages` until the task reaches `stopped`, `waiting`, or `error`. Returns the task object plus recent messages. Use `--verbose-messages` to include tool, plan, and explanation events while polling.

list.help: List tasks from the Manus v2 API.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of tasks to return

options.name: --table
options.help: Display tasks as a formatted table

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter: field:op:value

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

options.name: --cursor
options.help: Pagination cursor from a previous task list call

options.name: --order
options.help: Sort direction (asc or desc)

options.name: --scope
options.help: Task scope filter

options.name: --agent-id
options.help: Filter by agent ID

options.name: --project-id
options.help: Filter by project ID

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

list.usage_instructions: Use to list tasks from `task.list`. Supports the standard `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`, and `--table/-t` options, plus Manus cursor/order/scope filters such as `--cursor`, `--order`, `--scope`, `--agent-id`, and `--project-id`.

messages.help: List task event messages from the Manus v2 API.

arguments.name: task_id
arguments.help: Task ID to list messages for

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of messages to return

options.name: --table
options.help: Display messages as a formatted table

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter: field:op:value

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include (supports dot notation)

options.name: --cursor
options.help: Pagination cursor from a previous messages call

options.name: --order
options.help: Sort direction (asc or desc)

options.name: --verbose
options.help: Include detailed tool/plan/explanation events

options.name: --slides-format
options.help: Slide attachment format when applicable (html or pptx)

options.name: --download-files
options.help: Download message attachments returned by the API

options.name: --output-dir
options.help: Directory for downloaded files (default: current directory)

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

messages.usage_instructions: Use to list task event messages from `task.listMessages`. TASK_ID is required. Supports the standard `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`, and `--table/-t` options, plus Manus pagination and verbosity flags such as `--cursor`, `--order`, `--verbose`, and `--slides-format`. Use `--download-files` to download message attachments.

options.name: --title
options.help: New title for the task

options.name: --share-visibility
options.help: New task visibility (private, team, public)

options.name: --visible-in-task-list
options.help: Show the task in the Manus webapp task list

options.name: --hidden-in-task-list
options.help: Hide the task from the Manus webapp task list

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

update.usage_instructions: Use to update task metadata through `task.update`. TASK_ID is required and you must pass at least one mutation flag: `--title`, `--share-visibility`, `--visible-in-task-list`, or `--hidden-in-task-list`.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

stop.usage_instructions: Use to stop a running task through `task.stop`. TASK_ID is required. The API response is a lightweight acknowledgement; use `task wait` or `task get` afterward if you need the resulting task state.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

delete.usage_instructions: Use to permanently delete a task through `task.delete`. TASK_ID is required. Delete is separate from stop; stop a running task first if necessary.

confirm.help: Confirm a pending action so a waiting task can resume.

arguments.name: task_id
arguments.help: Task ID with the pending action

arguments.name: event_id
arguments.help: Waiting event ID from task messages

options.name: --input
options.help: Optional JSON input for the confirmation

options.name: --input-file
options.help: Read optional confirmation JSON input from a file

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name

confirm.usage_instructions: Use to confirm a waiting action through `task.confirmAction`. Pass the TASK_ID and the waiting EVENT_ID from `task messages`. Use `--input` or `--input-file` when the waiting event requires structured confirmation data.

task.usage_instructions: Manage Manus API v2 tasks. `create` starts a task, `send`/`continue` sends follow-up messages, `get` reads metadata, `messages` reads event history, `wait` polls `task.listMessages`, and `update`/`stop`/`delete`/`confirm` mutate task state. The injected task-group `--profile` option selects a saved auth profile; task `--agent-profile` selects Manus model capability.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `manus cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `manus cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

usage_instructions: Use `manus <command-group> <action> [arguments] [options]`. JSON is the default output. Use auth/profile `--profile` options for saved credential profiles; use task `--agent-profile` for Manus model selection. Consult command entries before executing.
